2605.29555.title: From Blind Guess to Informed Judgment: Teaching LLMs to Evaluate Materials by Building Knowledge-Augmented Preference Signals
2605.29555.paper_summary: As candidate generation and high-throughput experimentation advance, the primary bottleneck in materials discovery is shifting from property prediction to making reliable evaluations among massive candidate sets. We propose a Knowledge-Augmented Preference Signals Framework, MaterEval, that automatically produces, for the same candidate, two evaluations: an informed judgment that follows expert rules and provides supporting evidence, and a rule-removed blind guess. By pairing the two evaluations as preference data, we guide general-purpose large language models (LLMs), originally lacking materials-specific criteria, from intuitive judgment toward reliable evaluation supported by explicit evidence. To balance throughput, cost, and reliability, we further introduce a fast-slow reasoning scheme that decouples large-scale rapid screening from in-depth review on a small subset. Using high-entropy alloy (HEA) assessment as a case study, we show that, without external retrieval and relying solely on internalized capabilities, small open-source LLMs achieve substantial gains in accuracy, conclusion consistency, and evidence discrimination, approaching the performance of rule-based closed-source LLMs. These results demonstrate that expert rules can be systematically transformed into learnable preference signals, enabling a low-cost and deployable evaluation module for autonomous materials discovery loops.
2605.29555.paper_summary_zh: 隨著候選生成和高通量實驗的進步，材料發現的主要瓶頸正在從性質預測轉向在大量候選集之間進行可靠評估。我們提出了一個知識增強偏好信號框架，MaterEval，該框架自動為同一候選產生兩個評估：一個遵循專家規則並提供支持證據的知情判斷，以及一個去除規則的盲目猜測。通過將這兩個評估配對作為偏好數據，我們引導原本缺乏材料特定標準的一般性大型語言模型（LLMs），從直觀判斷轉向由明確證據支持的可靠評估。為了平衡通量、成本和可靠性，我們進一步引入了一種快慢推理方案，將大規模快速篩選與對小子集的深入審查解耦。以高熵合金（HEA）評估為案例研究，我們顯示，無需外部檢索，僅依賴內部化能力，小型開源LLMs在準確性、結論一致性和證據區別上取得了顯著提升，接近基於規則的閉源LLMs的性能。這些結果表明，專家規則可以系統地轉化為可學習的偏好信號，從而為自主材料發現循環提供一個低成本且可部署的評估模塊。
2605.29555.author: Yeyong Yu et.al.
2605.29555.authors: Yeyong Yu, Wenya Hu, Xing Wu, Quan Qian
